{{Quote|Elke wasn't just my bodyguard, Astraal! She was my ''teacher''! She was my ''friend''!|Princess Marasiah Fel|<ref name="leg2" />}}
'''Elke Vetter''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Imperial Knight]] who served the [[Fel Empire]] before [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] at the end of the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. In the proceeding [[Second Imperial Civil War|conflict]], she served as the [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] of Fel's [[Gender/Legends|daughter]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]], while the princess acted as her [[Parent/Legends|father's]] emissary and determined the loyalty of the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]]. Vetter was also the Marasiah's friend and master in the ways of the Imperial Knights.

In [[137 ABY]], Vetter was on [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] with the princess, who hid in an [[Imperial Mission]] [[Imperial Mission temple|temple]]. They were ambushed by [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Talon]], who had recently been promoted to the position of Krayt's [[Hand (One Sith)|Hand]]. In order to buy time for the princess's escape, Vetter [[Duel on Socorro|dueled]] with Talon; however, she was defeated by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Talon then used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to interrogate Vetter, learning vital information before [[Death/Legends|killing]] her with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Kill me if you will, Sith! The Princess has escaped!|Elke Vetter, to Darth Talon|<ref name="leg2" />}}
Elke Vetter, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Imperial Knight]], served the [[Fel Empire]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]] before [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|overthrew]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]. During the ensuing [[Second Imperial Civil War]], she served as the [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] of Fel's [[Gender/Legends|daughter]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]], while the princess both acted as her [[Parent/Legends|father's]] emissary and infiltrated planets in secret to discover which [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] remained loyal to him.<ref name="leg2" /> More than just Fel's bodyguard, Vetter was also the princess's master and friend, instructing her in the ways of the Imperial Knights.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|III|304|Vetter, Elke}}</ref>

In [[137 ABY]], Vetter was with Marasiah and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Imperial Mission]]ary [[Astraal Vao]], as they infiltrated the world of [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]]. Krayt ordered one of his [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]], the Twi'lek [[Darth Talon]]—who, as one of Krayt's most able servants, had recently been promoted to the position of [[Hand (One Sith)|Hand]]—to locate and kill [[Empire-in-exile|Emperor-in-exile]] Roan Fel. Planning to use the princess to find her father, Talon traveled to Socorro where the princess was hiding in the Imperial Mission's [[Imperial Mission temple|temple]]. Arriving on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], Talon razed the temple, setting it on fire. However, Vetter prevented the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] from getting to Princess Fel by engaging Talon in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Duel on Socorro|duel]] in order to give the princess and Astraal Vao enough time to escape the planet.<ref name="leg2" />

[[File:VetterTalon.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Vetter engaging Darth Talon]]
The duel ended when Vetter [[Cho mai/Legends|lost]] her left hand and was [[Shiak|stabbed]] through the right leg by Talon, before being kicked off a ledge. Defeated, Vetter told Talon that she could kill her, but the princess had escaped. Talon used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to interrogate Vetter, [[Drain Knowledge|pulling information from her mind]]. Talon used this information to develop a plan: she would let Marasiah lead her to her father or he would come to save her, and then Talon would kill both. Considering Vetter of no more interest, Talon unleashed a burst of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] the Imperial Knight.<ref name="leg2" />

While escaping, Princess Fel felt Vetter's death in the Force and instantly wanted revenge. Vao told the princess not to dishonor Vetter's sacrifice, managing to convince the princess that duty came first, and that their duty was to survive. The two then made their escape to the planet [[Vendaxa/Legends|Vendaxa]].<ref name="leg2" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Princess, Elke lived her life in the service of others.|Astraal Vao|<ref name="leg2" />}}
Elke Vetter was a Human with [[Color/Legends|blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a light skin tone. As an Imperial Knight, Vetter was loyal to Emperor Fel—whom she considered to be the true Emperor—above all else. She lived her life in the Empire's service, and was willing to give her life for it. When Talon ambushed the temple on Socorro, Vetter sacrificed herself to ensure Princess Marasiah Fel could escape—her final act of service to the Empire. During Talon's interrogation, Vetter was unable to prevent the Sith from breaking her will and pulling information from her mind, which Talon would use in her plan to kill Roan Fel.<ref name="leg2" />

==Powers and abilities==
An Imperial Knight, Elke Vetter was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and was trained in the arts of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. She wielded a [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|silver]]-bladed lightsaber in battle, with the [[Imperial Knight lightsaber|standard Imperial Knight hilt]]. However, despite her knowledge of lightsaber combat, she was unable to defeat Darth Talon in battle.<ref name="leg2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Elke Vetter made her first appearance in the second issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, [[Legacy (2006) 2|''Broken, Part 2'']], released in [[July]] [[2006]]. The issue was written by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]], as well as being penciled by Duursema.<ref name="leg2" />
